blocks.content:
Grafana Knowledge Graph automatically discovers and maps every entity in your environment -- services, nodes, pods, and more. In this guide you will explore two key features:

- **Entity catalog** -- browse, search, and inspect all discovered entities
- **RCA workbench** -- investigate issues across entities using insights and multiple visualization modes
You will learn how to browse discovered entities, filter by type and health, and drill into service-level details.
Navigate to the **Entity catalog**.

blocks.tooltip: Colored rings visualize health across five categories: Saturation, Amend, Anomaly, Failure, and Error.
blocks.content: Each entity in the catalog has insight rings -- colored circles that show its health status at a glance. The table also shows the entity name, technology stack, and key metrics like **Latency P95**, **Error Ratio**, and **Request Rate**.

blocks.tooltip: Search narrows the catalog to matching entities.
blocks.content: Use the search box to quickly find a specific entity by name.

### Entity types

The left sidebar lets you filter entities by type. The Knowledge Graph discovers many types of entities from your telemetry data.

blocks.tooltip: Services are the default view -- discovered from your metrics and traces.
blocks.content: **Service** is selected by default. Services represent application-level components discovered from metrics and traces. The count shows how many entities of each type exist.

blocks.tooltip: Switch to see infrastructure nodes instead of services.
blocks.content: Click **Node** to view infrastructure-level entities. Nodes represent the underlying compute instances running your services.

blocks.tooltip: Switch back to the service view.
blocks.content: Click **Service** to return to the service view.

### Insight rings

The colored rings next to each entity name represent its health status across five categories. These are the same insight categories used throughout Knowledge Graph.

blocks.tooltip: Filter to only show entities with error insights.
blocks.content:
Check **Error** under Insight Rings to filter the catalog to only entities experiencing errors. The five insight categories are:

- **Saturation** -- resource limits being reached
- **Amend** -- configuration changes detected
- **Anomaly** -- unusual metric behavior
- **Failure** -- component failures
- **Error** -- application errors

blocks.tooltip: Clear the filter to show all entities again.
blocks.content: Check **All** to clear the insight filter and show all entities.

### Drill into an entity

Clicking an entity name opens a detailed drawer with deep observability data. Let's explore what's inside.
Click the first entity to open its detail drawer.
Explore the entity detail drawer. Each tab gives you a different lens into this service's health and behavior.

steps.tooltip: Shows Latency, Error Ratio, Request Rate charts, duration distribution, and outbound dependencies.
Query and explore logs emitted by this service.
View distributed traces flowing through this service.
Browse all metrics associated with this service.
View metadata like cluster, namespace, workload type, and discovery timestamps.
Done exploring -- close the entity detail drawer.

blocks.content: You learned how to browse the Entity catalog, filter by entity type and insight category, and drill into individual entities for detailed observability data.
You will learn how to populate the RCA workbench, read insight timelines, drill into a service's KPI drawer, and explore multiple visualization modes.
Navigate to the **RCA workbench**.
The RCA (Root Cause Analysis) workbench is your investigation hub. You add entities here to compare their insights side-by-side and trace the chain of causation across your environment.

blocks.tooltip: Populates the workbench with the services that have the most active insights.
blocks.content: Click **Add top services by insights** to automatically populate the workbench with the most problematic services. This is the fastest way to start an investigation.

The workbench is now populated with services. The **Timeline** shows when insights fired for each entity -- red bars for errors, orange for warnings. Look for patterns: do errors correlate across services? Did one trigger the others?

blocks.tooltip: Shows each entity with its specific active insights listed.
blocks.content: Click **Summary** to see exactly which insights are firing per entity. Each service is listed with its assertions expanded below it.

### Expand and investigate a service

In the Summary view, each entity shows its active insights. You can collapse or expand entities to focus your investigation. Let's look closer at the **frontend** service.

blocks.tooltip: Collapse or expand to show/hide this service's insights.
blocks.content: Click **frontend** to toggle its insight list. Notice the red error icons and orange warning icons -- these tell you the severity at a glance.

### Open the KPI drawer

Every entity in the workbench has a KPI drawer -- a side panel with detailed dashboards for that service. The drawer is dynamically populated based on what the Knowledge Graph discovered: application metrics, Kubernetes details, traces, logs, and more. Hover over a service to reveal the action buttons.
Hover over the **frontend** service to reveal the action buttons, then open its KPI drawer.

steps.tooltip: Hover to reveal the action buttons: Causes, KPIs, Connected entities, and Remove.

steps.description: Click KPIs to open the drawer
steps.tooltip: Opens a detailed KPI drawer with dashboards auto-discovered for this service.

blocks.content: Explore the KPI drawer tabs. Each tab gives you a different lens into this service's health.

steps.tooltip: Shows Latency, Error Ratio, Request Rate, duration distribution, and outbound dependencies.
View distributed traces flowing through this service to find slow or errored requests.
Query and explore logs emitted by this service -- look for error stack traces.
Auto-discovered metadata: cluster, namespace, workload type, runtime, and discovery timestamps.
Return to the workbench to continue investigating.

blocks.content:
### Visualize the blast radius

The **Graph** tab shows a topology of how the entities in your workbench connect to each other. This is the same knowledge graph data powering the entire experience -- rendered as a real-time diagram with no manual diagramming required.

blocks.tooltip: Shows entity topology with nodes colored by health status.
blocks.content: Click **Graph** to see the service topology. Nodes are colored by health -- green for healthy, red/orange for impacted. Connections show dependencies so you can trace the blast radius of an issue.

### Summarize with the Mind map

The **Mind map** provides a hierarchical breakdown of all insights across every entity in your workbench, organized by category and severity.

blocks.tooltip: Tree-structured breakdown of all insights by category and severity.
blocks.content: Click **Mind map** to see the big picture. The root node shows total insight counts (critical, warning, info), branching into categories like **error** and **anomaly**. Expand nodes to drill down to individual assertions.

You learned how to populate the RCA workbench, read insight timelines, hover to reveal action buttons, drill into a service's KPI drawer with traces, logs, and metrics, and visualize blast radius with the Graph and Mind map views.

You have completed the Knowledge Graph tour. You now know how to:

- Browse and filter entities in the **Entity catalog**
- Drill into individual entities for detailed metrics, logs, and traces
- Use the **RCA workbench** to investigate issues across multiple entities
- Read insights across Timeline, Summary, Graph, and Mind map views

Next, try using the **Entity Graph** (found in the Observability menu) for a full visual topology of your environment, or set up **Health targets** to define SLOs for your services.
